steps.title: Notice what changes
steps.content: All four equations have the same intercept, $148.13$. The only meaningful difference is the slope, so use one far-right point on the displayed best-fit line to estimate the rise per $1$ unit of $x$.

steps.title: Estimate the slope
steps.content: The line is near $y=735$ when $x=10$. Starting from the intercept $148.13$, the rise is about $735-148.13=586.87$ over a run of $10$, so the slope is about $58.7$.

steps.title: Reject the low-slope choices
steps.content: Graph the choices that should fail near the readable point $(10,735)$. Choices A and B are almost flat, and choice C is still too low near $x=10$. None of those reaches the height shown by the best-fit line.

steps.title: Confirm the matching slope
steps.content: Choice D reaches about $734$ at $x=10$: $148.13+58.61(10)=734.23$. That matches the drawn line and the estimated slope from the regression check.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The slope closest to $58.7$ is $58.61$, so the best representation is $y=148.13+58.61x$. That is choice D.
